description: API behavior: client portal SSO discovery returns ok with no providers when tenant context is missing or invalid.
API behavior: client portal SSO discovery returns configured providers when tenant context resolves from tenant slug.
API behavior: client portal SSO discovery returns configured providers when tenant context resolves from portal domain.
API behavior: client portal SSO resolve rejects provider, callback, tenant, or discovery-state mismatches with generic failure.
Auth behavior: client portal OAuth mapping authenticates an active client user only when tenant and user type match.
Auth behavior: client portal OAuth sign-in redirects through vanity-domain handoff for a client user with active portal domain.
UI behavior: ClientLoginForm keeps SSO unavailable before tenant context and enables discovery only after tenant context is present.
Regression behavior: existing client portal credentials login still signs in and redirects as before.
Regression behavior: existing MSP SSO discovery and resolve behavior remains unchanged after shared component/helper changes.
Settings behavior: SSO settings can persist autoLinkClient, MSP workspace default client portal Entra provisioning mode, and entitlement-removal deactivation independently.
DB-backed integration: Entra sync creates or links a contact but skips portal user provisioning when provisioning mode is disabled.
DB-backed integration: Entra sync skips portal user provisioning when no entitlement group is configured or the user lacks group membership.
DB-backed integration: built-in mode provisions a client portal user and Microsoft OAuth account link for an enabled entitled Entra user after non-ambiguous contact reconciliation.
DB-backed integration: Entra sync detects conflicting portal users and records a skipped/conflict outcome without creating a duplicate user.
DB-backed integration: entitlement removal deactivates an Entra-managed portal user when configured.
DB-backed integration: disabled Entra account deactivates the corresponding Entra-managed portal user.
DB-backed integration: entitlement removal does not deactivate a manually managed portal user.
DB-backed integration: entitlement return reactivates only lifecycle-deactivated Entra-managed portal users, not manually disabled users.
Invitation behavior: invitation with Entra pre-link completes through OAuth without requiring password setup.
Configuration behavior: selected entitlement group must belong to the managed Entra tenant and group membership mode defaults to transitive.
DB-backed integration: newly Entra-provisioned portal users in built-in mode receive the configured default role, and later manual role changes are preserved across sync runs.
UI behavior: selecting a broad Entra entitlement group presents warning copy that all enabled users in the group become eligible for portal access.
DB-backed integration: workflow-managed mode publishes an idempotent eligible-access workflow event for an entitled Entra user and does not create or link a portal user directly.

DB-backed integration: workflow-managed mode publishes an idempotent access-removal workflow event when entitlement is removed or the Entra account is disabled and does not directly deactivate the portal user.
Workflow behavior: portal user workflow actions create/link users, assign roles, upsert Microsoft OAuth links, and deactivate/reactivate access idempotently without affecting manually managed users.
UI behavior: workflow-managed mode explains that the selected workflow owns provisioning, role assignment, invitations, and lifecycle changes.
UI behavior: security SSO settings expose separate auto-link toggles for internal users and client portal users, persisting autoLinkClient independently.
Settings behavior: SSO preferences default client portal Entra provisioning mode to disabled, default entitlement-removal deactivation to true, and expose UI controls for both values.

DB-backed integration: Entra sync resolves effective portal provisioning mode from per-client override first and MSP workspace default second, and skips provisioning/events when the effective mode is disabled.

UI/API behavior: client mapping configuration supports inherit/disabled/built-in/workflow-managed override choices, keeps portal access group explicit per managed Microsoft tenant, and uses MSP workspace / managed Microsoft tenant terminology.
DB-backed integration: built-in provisioning uses per-client default role override when present, otherwise workspace default role, otherwise User, while still preserving later manual role changes.
